blocks.body: Passive Infinitives and Gerunds are complex passive constructions that are often used in academic, scientific, and formal writing. Understanding these forms will help you better understand complex texts and write more professionally.

{ "title": "7. Key rules", "items": [ "to be + V3 is passive infinitive for present/future actions.", "to have been + V3 is a perfect passive infinitive for previous actions.", "being + V3 is a passive gerund after prepositions and verbs of feeling.", "having been + V3 is the perfect passive gerund for causal relationships.", "These forms are typical of academic and formal style.", "Use to be done after need, want, expect, seem, appear.", "Use being done after like, hate, enjoy, avoid and prepositions." ] }
